title: Stale transcription text displays after re-recording a voice memo
symptom: After deleting and re-recording a voice memo with the same name, the old transcription text appears instead of the new one.

environment.additional: iCloud sync enabled, SQLite state DB present from prior session

stepsToReproduce: Record a voice memo named 'Shopping List' on iPhone
Wait for Utterd to detect and transcribe it (memo status becomes 'transcribed')
Delete 'Shopping List' from Voice Memos on iPhone
Record a new voice memo also named 'Shopping List' with different spoken content
Wait for iCloud sync to deliver the new file to the Mac
Observe: Utterd shows the old transcription text, not the new one
expectedBehavior: Utterd detects the new file as a distinct memo (different content hash or modification timestamp) and transcribes it fresh, displaying the new transcription.
actualBehavior: Utterd matches the file path to the existing state tracker record and serves the cached transcription from the first recording. The daemon log shows 'File already tracked, skipping: ~/Library/Mobile Documents/.../Shopping List.m4a'. The state tracker does not check content hash or modification timestamp on re-detection.
impact: Any user who re-records a memo at the same file path will see stale transcription text. No workaround short of manually deleting the SQLite state database and restarting the daemon.
evidence: SQLite query: SELECT * FROM memos WHERE file_path LIKE '%Shopping%' returns single row with original content_hash
Daemon log: 'File already tracked, skipping: ~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~VoiceMemos/Recordings/Shopping List.m4a'
The .m4a file's modification timestamp is newer than the state tracker's recorded timestamp

targetRepositories.name: utterd
targetRepositories.area: State tracker module — duplicate detection logic in the memo processing pipeline
